name: Sudarshana Suri
dates_notes: Pre-Vedānta-Deśika; dated by manuscript and citation chronology to c. 1230 CE.
core_thesis: Sudarśana Sūri is the principal *vyākhyātṛ* of Rāmānuja's *Śrī-Bhāṣya* and the figure through whom the school's exegetical tradition is stabilised in the generation between Rāmānuja and Vedānta Deśika. The contribution is interpretive rather than doctrinal: he proposes no new thesis. What he does — and what makes him indispensable — is settle, at contested point after contested point, what the *Śrī-Bhāṣya* actually says. The settlements concern four nodes. First, the precise force of each step of Rāmānuja's *sapta-vidhānupapatti* against *avidyā* (*Śruta-Prakāśikā* on [*Śruta-Prakāśikā* on *Śrī-Bhāṣya* 1.1.1](cite://sudarsana/shruta-prakashika/1.1.1), *Mahā-Pūrvapakṣa* and *Mahā-Siddhānta*), including the Advaitin replies the *Śrī-Bhāṣya* leaves implicit. Second, the construal of *sāmānādhikaraṇya* in *tat tvam asi* on the body-soul model (*Śruta-Prakāśikā* on [*Śruta-Prakāśikā* on *Śrī-Bhāṣya* 1.1.1](cite://sudarsana/shruta-prakashika/1.1.1), *Mahā-Siddhānta*; cf. on 1.4.22), against alternative readings (numerical identity, *aupādhika* difference, *bhedābheda*) that the bare *Bhāṣya* does not always rule out word-by-word. Third, the *jīva*–*Īśvara* relation under the *śarīra-śarīrin* construal (*Śruta-Prakāśikā* on [*Śruta-Prakāśikā* on *Śrī-Bhāṣya* 2.1.9; 2.3.43](cite://sudarsana/shruta-prakashika/2.1.9-2.3.43), on the *jīvāṃśa* sūtra), particularly how *apṛthak-siddhi* differs from inherence (*samavāya*) and from mere conjunction. Fourth, the relation between *bhakti-yoga* and *prapatti* as *upāya*-s (*Śruta-Prakāśikā* on [*Śruta-Prakāśikā* on *Śrī-Bhāṣya* 3.2.24; 4.1.1–2](cite://sudarsana/shruta-prakashika/3.2.24-4.1.1-2)). Sudarśana stands prior to the formal Vaḍakalai-Tenkalai split, and so both schools claim him authoritatively. The constraint runs the other way: his readings determine what later Vaḍakalai (Vedānta Deśika) and Tenkalai (Pillai-Lokācārya) can say while still claiming to read the *Śrī-Bhāṣya*.

Sudarśana Sūri is the principal *vyākhyātṛ* of Rāmānuja's *Śrī-Bhāṣya* and the figure through whom the school's exegetical tradition is stabilised in the generation between Rāmānuja and Vedānta Deśika. The contribution is interpretive rather than doctrinal: he proposes no new thesis. What he does — and what makes him indispensable — is settle, at contested point after contested point, what the *Śrī-Bhāṣya* actually says. The settlements concern four nodes. First, the precise force of each step of Rāmānuja's *sapta-vidhānupapatti* against *avidyā*[on the *Mahā-Pūrvapakṣa* and *Mahā-Siddhānta*](cite://sudarsana/shruta-prakashika/1.1.1), including the Advaitin replies the *Śrī-Bhāṣya* leaves implicit. Second, the construal of *sāmānādhikaraṇya* in *tat tvam asi* on the body-soul model[on the *Mahā-Siddhānta* at 1.1.1](cite://sudarsana/shruta-prakashika/1.1.1), against alternative readings (numerical identity, *aupādhika* difference, *bhedābheda*) that the bare *Bhāṣya* does not always rule out word-by-word. Third, the *jīva*–*Īśvara* relation under the *śarīra-śarīrin* construal[on the *jīvāṃśa* sūtra at 2.1.9 and 2.3.43](cite://sudarsana/shruta-prakashika/2.1.9-2.3.43), particularly how *apṛthak-siddhi* differs from inherence (*samavāya*) and from mere conjunction. Fourth, the relation between *bhakti-yoga* and *prapatti* as *upāya*-s[on 3.2.24 and 4.1.1–2](cite://sudarsana/shruta-prakashika/3.2.24-4.1.1-2). Sudarśana stands prior to the formal Vaḍakalai-Tenkalai split, and so both schools claim him authoritatively. The constraint runs the other way: his readings determine what later Vaḍakalai (Vedānta Deśika) and Tenkalai (Pillai-Lokācārya) can say while still claiming to read the *Śrī-Bhāṣya*.

engaged_works.title: Shruta-Prakashika on Shri-Bhashya
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored sub-commentary on the Śrī-Bhāṣya.
engaged_works.summary: The school's canonical sub-commentary (*vyākhyā*) on the *Śrī-Bhāṣya*, traditionally composed at Rāmānuja's own request. Its authority is such that Vedānta Deśika's *Tātparya-Candrikā* takes the *Śruta-Prakāśikā* as its base text, glossing it rather than the *Śrī-Bhāṣya* directly — a sign of how thoroughly the school sees Rāmānuja through Sudarśana's lens. The work fixes the school's reading at every contested passage of the *Mahā-Siddhānta* on *sūtra* 1.1.1. The *Mahā-Pūrvapakṣa*'s statement of Advaita is delimited so that the strongest concessions are clearly marked; each step of the *sapta-vidhānupapatti* is given its precise dialectical force (*Śruta-Prakāśikā* on [*Śruta-Prakāśikā* on *Śrī-Bhāṣya* 1.1.1](cite://sudarsana/shruta-prakashika/1.1.1), *Mahā-Siddhānta*), including the Advaitin counter-moves Rāmānuja leaves implicit. The *sāmānādhikaraṇya* of *tat tvam asi* is fixed on the body-soul model (*Śruta-Prakāśikā* on [*Śruta-Prakāśikā* on *Śrī-Bhāṣya* 1.1.1](cite://sudarsana/shruta-prakashika/1.1.1); on 1.4.22), against numerical identity, *aupādhika* difference, and *bhedābheda* readings. The doctrine of *dharmi-bhūta-jñāna* implicit in Rāmānuja's epistemology is made explicit (*Śruta-Prakāśikā* on [*Śruta-Prakāśikā* on *Śrī-Bhāṣya* 1.1.1](cite://sudarsana/shruta-prakashika/1.1.1) and 2.3.18, on the *jīva* as *jñātṛ*). The *adhikaraṇa*-divisions are stabilised, and the unnamed *pūrvapakṣa*-positions Rāmānuja is targeting at condensed points are identified (e.g., *Śruta-Prakāśikā* on [*Śruta-Prakāśikā* on *Śrī-Bhāṣya* 2.2.1–32](cite://sudarsana/shruta-prakashika/2.2.1-32), identifying the rival darśanas refuted). The work matters because every subsequent Viśiṣṭādvaita engagement with the *Brahma-Sūtra* — Vaḍakalai and Tenkalai alike — operates through the *Śruta-Prakāśikā*'s settlements.
